    def open_resource(
        self, resource: str, mode: str = "rb", *, errors: str | None = None
    ) -> t.IO[t.AnyStr]:
        """Open a resource file relative to :attr:`root_path` for
        reading.

        For example, if the file ``schema.sql`` is next to the file
        ``app.py`` where the ``Flask`` app is defined, it can be opened
        with:

        .. code-block:: python

            with app.open_resource("schema.sql") as f:
                conn.executescript(f.read())

        :param resource: Path to the resource relative to
            :attr:`root_path`.
        :param mode: Open the file in this mode. Only reading is
            supported, valid values are "r" (or "rt") and "rb".
        :param errors: The error handling scheme to use for encoding errors.
            The default is ``None``, which uses the default error handling.
            For text modes ("r", "rt"), this is passed to :func:`open`.
            For binary mode ("rb"), this parameter is ignored.

        Note this is a duplicate of the same method in the Flask
        class.

        .. versionchanged:: 3.1
            Added the ``errors`` parameter.
        """
        if mode not in {"r", "rt", "rb"}:
            raise ValueError("Resources can only be opened for reading.")

        return open(os.path.join(self.root_path, resource), mode, errors=errors)
EOF

        """
        if mode not in {"r", "rt", "rb"}:
            raise ValueError("Resources can only be opened for reading.")

        return open(os.path.join(self.root_path, resource), mode)'''

        self, resource: str, mode: str = "rb", encoding: str | None = None

        """Open a resource file relative to :attr:`root_path` for
        reading.

        For example, if the file ``schema.sql`` is next to the file
        ``app.py`` where the ``Flask`` app is defined, it can be opened
        with:

        .. code-block:: python

            with app.open_resource("schema.sql") as f:
                conn.executescript(f.read())

        :param resource: Path to the resource relative to
            :attr:`root_path`.
        :param mode: Open the file in this mode. Only reading is
            supported, valid values are "r" (or "rt") and "rb".
        :param encoding: The encoding to use when opening the file in
            text mode. If not given, defaults to "utf-8" for text mode.

        Note this is a duplicate of the same method in the Flask
        class.

        """
        if mode not in {"r", "rt", "rb"}:
            raise ValueError("Resources can only be opened for reading.")

        if mode in {"r", "rt"} and encoding is None:
            encoding = "utf-8"

        return open(os.path.join(self.root_path, resource), mode, encoding=encoding)'''

# Update open_instance_resource method
old_open_instance_resource = '''    def open_instance_resource(self, resource: str, mode: str = "rb") -> t.IO[t.AnyStr]:
        """Opens a resource from the application's instance folder
        (:attr:`instance_path`).  Otherwise works like
        :meth:`open_resource`.  Instance resources can also be opened for
        writing.

        :param resource: the name of the resource.  To access resources within
                         subfolders use forward slashes as separator.
        :param mode: resource file opening mode, default is 'rb'.
        """
        return open(os.path.join(self.instance_path, resource), mode)'''

        self, resource: str, mode: str = "rb", encoding: str | None = None
    ) -> t.IO[t.AnyStr]:
        """Opens a resource from the application's instance folder

        writing.

        :param resource: the name of the resource.  To access resources within

        :param mode: resource file opening mode, default is 'rb'.
        :param encoding: The encoding to use when opening the file in
            text mode. If not given, defaults to "utf-8" for text mode.
        """
        if mode in {"r", "rt"} and encoding is None:
            encoding = "utf-8"

        return open(os.path.join(self.instance_path, resource), mode, encoding=encoding)'''

content = content.replace(old_open_instance_resource, new_open_instance_resource)

# Write the file

    f.write(content)

print("app.py updated successfully")
EOF

    def open_resource(
        self, resource: str, mode: str = "rb", *, errors: str | None = None
    ) -> t.IO[t.AnyStr]:
        """Open a resource file relative to :attr:`root_path` for
        reading.

        For example, if the file ``schema.sql`` is next to the file
        ``app.py`` where the ``Flask`` app is defined, it can be opened
        with:

        .. code-block:: python

            with app.open_resource("schema.sql") as f:
                conn.executescript(f.read())

        :param resource: Path to the resource relative to
            :attr:`root_path`.
        :param mode: Open the file in this mode. Only reading is
            supported, valid values are "r" (or "rt") and "rb".
        :param errors: The error handling scheme to use for encoding errors.
            The default is ``None``, which uses the default error handling.
            For text modes ("r", "rt"), this is passed to :func:`open`.
            For binary mode ("rb"), this parameter is ignored.

        Note this is a duplicate of the same method in the Flask
        class.

        .. versionchanged:: 3.1
            Added the ``errors`` parameter.
        """
        if mode not in {"r", "rt", "rb"}:
            raise ValueError("Resources can only be opened for reading.")

        return open(os.path.join(self.root_path, resource), mode, errors=errors)

cat > /tmp/open_resource_combined.py << 'EOF'
    def open_resource(
        self,
        resource: str,
        mode: str = "rb",
        *,
        encoding: str | None = None,
        errors: str | None = None,
    ) -> t.IO[t.AnyStr]:
        """Open a resource file relative to :attr:`root_path` for
        reading.

        For example, if the file ``schema.sql`` is next to the file
        ``app.py`` where the ``Flask`` app is defined, it can be opened
        with:

        .. code-block:: python

            with app.open_resource("schema.sql") as f:
                conn.executescript(f.read())

        :param resource: Path to the resource relative to
            :attr:`root_path`.
        :param mode: Open the file in this mode. Only reading is
            supported, valid values are "r" (or "rt") and "rb".
        :param encoding: The encoding to use when reading the file in text
            mode. The default is "utf-8". For binary mode ("rb"), this
            parameter is ignored.
        :param errors: The error handling scheme to use for encoding errors.
            The default is ``None``, which uses the default error handling.
            For text modes ("r", "rt"), this is passed to :func:`open`.
            For binary mode ("rb"), this parameter is ignored.

        Note this is a duplicate of the same method in the Flask
        class.

        .. versionchanged:: 3.1
            Added the ``encoding`` and ``errors`` parameters.
        """
        if mode not in {"r", "rt", "rb"}:
            raise ValueError("Resources can only be opened for reading.")

        kwargs = {}
        if mode in {"r", "rt"}:
            if encoding is not None:
                kwargs["encoding"] = encoding
            if errors is not None:
                kwargs["errors"] = errors

        return open(os.path.join(self.root_path, resource), mode, **kwargs)
EOF
